description:
GOAL: Heap-allocated vectors with raw contiguous payloads. SIMD-friendly. Unblocks fz-ul4.14 (SIMD codegen).

## Decisions captured

- Per-element-kind: HeapKind::VecI64 / VecF64 / VecU8 / VecBit. Kind is in the header, NOT a discriminant byte in the payload (keeps payload pure raw data, faster SIMD).
- Layout: HeapHeader (16) + len:u32 (4) + padding (4) + raw_payload (16-byte aligned for SIMD).
- NON-MOVING collector (.11.2 design choice) ensures stable addresses — SIMD codegen in .14 can rely on this.
- Builtins (already in interp): vec_get / vec_map / vec_reduce / etc. Wire to IR prims.
- ~v[1, 2, 3] / ~b[0xff] / ~bits[1, 0, 1, 1] sigils -> Prim::AllocVec(kind, [elements]).

## Deliverables

- IR prims for vec alloc / get / per-kind ops.
- Codegen for each kind.
- Tracer branches for VecI64/F64/U8/Bit in .11.2 (no FzValue inside; nothing to trace, just account for size).

## Tests

- `~v[1, 2, 3]` allocates a Vec(I64).
- `~v[1.0, 2.0]` allocates a Vec(F64).
- `~b[0xff, 0xab]` allocates a Vec(U8).
- `~bits[1, 0, 1, 1]` allocates a Vec(Bit).
- vec_map and vec_reduce work via JIT (currently interp-only via builtins).
- Pinned-stable address: allocate + GC + read still works (proves non-moving GC).
- Existing vec fixtures (from main.rs SAMPLE constant) work via JIT.

## Unblocks

- fz-ul4.14 SIMD codegen.
title: Vec types as heap types (unblocks .14 SIMD)
